title: Versatile Readers
description: You are given three tables: library members, books with genres, and borrow records. Return names of 'premium' members who have borrowed books from at least 2 different genres. Order alphabetically.
inputFormat:
members(id, name, membership_type)
books(id, title, genre)
borrows(id, member_id, book_id, returned)
outputFormat: One member name per line, ordered alphabetically.
constraints: Use JOIN, GROUP BY, and HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT genre) >= 2. Filter for premium membership. Include ORDER BY name. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
sampleSolution: SELECT m.name FROM members m JOIN borrows b ON m.id = b.member_id JOIN books bk ON b.book_id = bk.id WHERE m.membership_type = 'premium' GROUP BY m.id, m.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT bk.genre) >= 2 ORDER BY m.name;
hint: Join all three tables, filter by membership_type, then use GROUP BY + HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT bk.genre) >= 2.

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE members (id INTEGER, name TEXT, membership_type TEXT); CREATE TABLE books (id INTEGER, title TEXT, genre TEXT); CREATE TABLE borrows (id INTEGER, member_id INTEGER, book_id INTEGER, returned INTEGER); INSERT INTO members VALUES (1,'Alice','premium'),(2,'Bob','standard'),(3,'Charlie','premium'),(4,'Diana','premium'); INSERT INTO books VALUES (1,'Dune','SciFi'),(2,'Foundation','SciFi'),(3,'1984','Dystopia'),(4,'Neuromancer','SciFi'),(5,'Brave New World','Dystopia'); INSERT INTO borrows VALUES (1,1,1,1),(2,1,3,1),(3,2,1,0),(4,2,3,0),(5,3,1,1),(6,3,2,0),(7,4,3,1),(8,4,5,1);

CREATE TABLE members (id INTEGER, name TEXT, membership_type TEXT); CREATE TABLE books (id INTEGER, title TEXT, genre TEXT); CREATE TABLE borrows (id INTEGER, member_id INTEGER, book_id INTEGER, returned INTEGER); INSERT INTO members VALUES (1,'Eve','premium'),(2,'Frank','premium'),(3,'Grace','standard'),(4,'Hank','premium'); INSERT INTO books VALUES (1,'The Hobbit','Fantasy'),(2,'Harry Potter','Fantasy'),(3,'The Martian','SciFi'),(4,'Sherlock Holmes','Mystery'),(5,'Cosmos','Science'); INSERT INTO borrows VALUES (1,1,1,1),(2,1,3,1),(3,1,4,0),(4,2,1,1),(5,2,2,0),(6,3,3,1),(7,3,4,1),(8,4,3,1),(9,4,5,1);

CREATE TABLE members (id INTEGER, name TEXT, membership_type TEXT); CREATE TABLE books (id INTEGER, title TEXT, genre TEXT); CREATE TABLE borrows (id INTEGER, member_id INTEGER, book_id INTEGER, returned INTEGER); INSERT INTO members VALUES (1,'Iris','premium'),(2,'Jack','standard'); INSERT INTO books VALUES (1,'Novel A','Fiction'),(2,'Novel B','Fiction'); INSERT INTO borrows VALUES (1,1,1,1),(2,1,2,0),(3,2,1,1);

CREATE TABLE members (id INTEGER, name TEXT, membership_type TEXT); CREATE TABLE books (id INTEGER, title TEXT, genre TEXT); CREATE TABLE borrows (id INTEGER, member_id INTEGER, book_id INTEGER, returned INTEGER); INSERT INTO members VALUES (1,'Kate','premium'),(2,'Leo','premium'),(3,'Mia','premium'); INSERT INTO books VALUES (1,'Romance A','Romance'),(2,'Thriller A','Thriller'),(3,'Mystery A','Mystery'),(4,'SciFi A','SciFi'); INSERT INTO borrows VALUES (1,1,1,1),(2,1,2,1),(3,2,2,1),(4,2,3,1),(5,3,3,1),(6,3,4,1);

CREATE TABLE members (id INTEGER, name TEXT, membership_type TEXT); CREATE TABLE books (id INTEGER, title TEXT, genre TEXT); CREATE TABLE borrows (id INTEGER, member_id INTEGER, book_id INTEGER, returned INTEGER); INSERT INTO members VALUES (1,'Noah','premium'),(2,'Olivia','basic'); INSERT INTO books VALUES (1,'History',  'History'),(2,'Geography','Geography'),(3,'Science','Science'); INSERT INTO borrows VALUES (1,1,1,1),(2,1,2,0),(3,2,1,1),(4,2,2,1),(5,2,3,1);
